description: Deep review and fix all mismatches between the docs site components/styles and the original RetroUI source code in ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/. Apply the purple theme with exact CSS variables. Every element must match RetroUI's NeoBrutalism design system: border-2 border-black (not border-border), @theme inline, correct button sizing, proper press effects, and no visual bugs like invisible card titles.

userStories.title: Fix @theme inline and add missing theme variables
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the docs CSS @theme block to match RetroUI's global.css exactly so that Tailwind utility classes resolve identically.
acceptanceCriteria: @theme inline block includes `--chart-1` through `--chart-5` variables: `--chart-1: var(--chart-1);` etc.
bun run build passes (from docs/ directory: cd docs && bunx astro build)
userStories.notes: File: ~/workspace/src/opentabs/docs/src/styles/global.css, line 9. Change `@theme {` to `@theme inline {`. Then add after line 37 (before the closing `}`): `--background-image: var(--background-image);` and `--chart-1: var(--chart-1);` through `--chart-5: var(--chart-5);`. Reference: ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/app/global.css lines 4-38. Note: The `@theme inline` keyword prevents theme values from being emitted as CSS custom properties — they're only available to Tailwind utility classes. This matches RetroUI's approach. IMPORTANT: The docs site uses Starlight + Tailwind via @astrojs/starlight-tailwind, which may not support `@theme inline` the same way. If the build breaks with `@theme inline`, keep `@theme` (without inline) and document why. The build command for the docs site is `cd ~/workspace/src/opentabs/docs && npx astro build` (or `bunx astro build`). The root `bun run build` uses tsc --build which does NOT build the docs site.

userStories.title: Add missing --background-image and --chart-* to :root and .dark blocks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the :root and .dark CSS variable blocks to include all variables from RetroUI's .theme-purple so nothing is missing.
acceptanceCriteria: .dark block includes `--background-image: url('/images/bg_void_3.svg');` (same as light for theme-purple since RetroUI uses this value)
.dark block includes same chart variables as :root (RetroUI .dark .theme-purple does not override chart values, so they inherit from .theme-purple)
All existing CSS variables in :root and .dark remain unchanged
userStories.notes: File: ~/workspace/src/opentabs/docs/src/styles/global.css. Add to :root block (after line 57, before closing `}`): `--background-image: url('/images/bg_void_3.svg');` and `--chart-1: #ffdb33;` through `--chart-5: #e63946;`. The .dark .theme-purple in RetroUI (lines 120-137 of RetroUI global.css) does NOT override chart or background-image, so they inherit from .theme-purple. Add the same values to .dark block for explicitness. Reference: ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/app/global.css lines 95-137.

userStories.title: Fix card title color bug — text-card-foreground is invisible in light mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see feature card titles on the homepage so they are readable in both light and dark mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Feature card h3 elements in index.mdx do NOT use `text-card-foreground` class
Feature card h3 elements use inherited text color (matching RetroUI Card behavior where CardTitle inherits foreground color)
Card titles are clearly visible and readable in both light and dark mode
userStories.notes: File: ~/workspace/src/opentabs/docs/src/content/docs/index.mdx. On lines 18, 24, 30, 36, 42, 48 — every `<h3>` has `text-card-foreground`. In light mode, --card-foreground is #f5f5f5 (near-white), making titles invisible on white cards. RetroUI Card.tsx CardTitle (line 30-31) does NOT set text color — it uses `Text as='h3'` which inherits foreground. Remove `text-card-foreground` from all h3 elements. They should just be `<h3 class="font-head text-lg">`. The text will inherit --foreground (#1a1a1a in light, #f5f5f5 in dark) which is always readable.

userStories.title: Fix Hero.astro buttons to match RetroUI Button.tsx exactly
userStories.description: As a designer, I want the hero buttons to match RetroUI's Button component patterns exactly — flex (not inline-flex), responsive lg sizing, and border-black.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero button base class uses `flex` instead of `inline-flex` (matching RetroUI Button.tsx line 7)
Hero primary button uses `border-black` instead of `border-border` (matching RetroUI Button.tsx default variant line 12)
Hero buttons use responsive lg sizing: `px-6 lg:px-8 py-2 lg:py-3 text-md lg:text-lg` (matching RetroUI Button.tsx lg size line 23)

Hero secondary (outline) button uses `border-black` — but since outline variant in RetroUI does not specify border color, it should just use `border-2` without explicit color (inherits currentColor) OR use `border-black` for consistency
Hero buttons include `disabled:opacity-60 disabled:cursor-not-allowed` in base (matching RetroUI Button.tsx line 7)
userStories.notes: File: ~/workspace/src/opentabs/docs/src/overrides/Hero.astro, line 20. Current class string: `font-head transition-all rounded outline-hidden cursor-pointer duration-200 font-medium inline-flex justify-center items-center shadow-md hover:shadow active:shadow-none border-2 border-border hover:translate-y-1 active:translate-y-2 active:translate-x-1 px-6 py-3 text-lg no-underline`. Changes needed: (1) `inline-flex` → `flex` (RetroUI Button.tsx line 7 uses `flex`). (2) `border-border` → `border-black` (RetroUI Button.tsx line 12 uses `border-black`). (3) `px-6 py-3 text-lg` → `px-6 lg:px-8 py-2 lg:py-3 text-md lg:text-lg` (RetroUI lg size, line 23). (4) Add `disabled:opacity-60 disabled:cursor-not-allowed` to base. Reference: ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/Button.tsx. Note: The hero-actions container is `display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; justify-content: center;` so `flex` on the buttons (block-level flex) will still work since they're flex children. However, if this causes buttons to stretch full-width, we may need to add `w-auto` or keep `inline-flex`. Test visually.

userStories.title: Fix Pagination.astro buttons to match RetroUI Button.tsx exactly
userStories.description: As a designer, I want the pagination buttons to use border-black matching RetroUI's Button component.
acceptanceCriteria: Both buttons include `disabled:opacity-60 disabled:cursor-not-allowed` in class string
userStories.notes: File: ~/workspace/src/opentabs/docs/src/overrides/Pagination.astro. Line 18 (prev button): change `border-border` to `border-black`. Line 34 (next button): change `border-border` to `border-black`. Add `disabled:opacity-60 disabled:cursor-not-allowed` to both. Reference: RetroUI Button.tsx uses `border-2 border-black` on default and secondary variants.

userStories.title: Fix ThemeSelect.astro active state — add missing translateX(1px)
userStories.description: As a designer, I want the theme toggle press effect to match all other interactive elements and RetroUI's button pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: ThemeSelect active state includes both translateY(2px) AND translateX(1px)
This matches Hero.astro buttons (`active:translate-y-2 active:translate-x-1`) and RetroUI Button.tsx pattern
userStories.notes: File: ~/workspace/src/opentabs/docs/src/overrides/ThemeSelect.astro, line 27. Current: `transform: translateY(2px);`. Change to: `transform: translateY(2px) translateX(1px);`. This matches the pattern used by Hero.astro, Pagination.astro, search button (line 292 of global.css), menu button (line 337), and social icons (line 357).

userStories.title: Normalize shadow token usage — use CSS variables consistently
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all shadow values in global.css to use the CSS variable tokens (--shadow-sm, --shadow-md, etc.) instead of hardcoding the pixel values.
acceptanceCriteria: Search button uses `var(--shadow-sm)` instead of `2px 2px 0 0 var(--border)` (line 280)
Search button hover uses `var(--shadow-xs)` instead of `1px 1px 0 0 var(--border)` (line 286)
Menu button uses `var(--shadow-sm)` instead of `2px 2px 0 0 var(--border)` (line 325)
Menu button hover uses `var(--shadow-xs)` instead of `1px 1px 0 0 var(--border)` (line 331)
Social icons use `var(--shadow-sm)` instead of `2px 2px 0 0 var(--border)` (line 344)
Social icons hover uses `var(--shadow-xs)` instead of `1px 1px 0 0 var(--border)` (line 351)
Search modal uses `var(--shadow-lg)` instead of `6px 6px 0 0 var(--border)` (line 305)
Search input uses `var(--shadow-md)` instead of `4px 4px 0 0 var(--border)` (line 312)
Search input focus uses `var(--shadow-xs)` instead of `1px 1px 0 0 var(--border)` (line 318)
Code blocks use `var(--shadow-md)` instead of `4px 4px 0 0 var(--border)` (line 239)
ThemeSelect already uses `var(--shadow-sm)` — no change needed
userStories.notes: File: ~/workspace/src/opentabs/docs/src/styles/global.css. Replace all hardcoded shadow pixel values with the corresponding CSS variable token. The shadow scale is: --shadow-xs = 1px, --shadow-sm = 2px, --shadow = 3px, --shadow-md = 4px, --shadow-lg = 6px, --shadow-xl = 10px, --shadow-2xl = 16px. IMPORTANT: Because we're using `@theme inline`, the shadow variables defined in the @theme block are NOT emitted as CSS custom properties. They are only available to Tailwind utility classes (shadow-sm, shadow-md, etc.). The `var(--shadow-sm)` references in plain CSS (like in ThemeSelect.astro) reference the values set in :root or @theme — but with `@theme inline`, these won't be available as CSS custom properties. This means we CANNOT use `var(--shadow-sm)` in plain CSS if @theme is inline. If US-001 changes to `@theme inline`, then this story must keep the hardcoded values OR define the shadow scale separately in :root. If US-001 keeps `@theme` (without inline), then this story can proceed with var() references. Check the outcome of US-001 first. If @theme inline is used, SKIP this story and keep hardcoded values.
